posture: Quiet scan day — no free-source innovation signals could be confidently dated to the last 24 hours; sector momentum remains in waste/circular economy, AI-assisted planning and smart-community AI, but nothing new broke in-window.
summary: Empty day. A full scan of the free sources in §1a (AU councils & sector), §1b (AU industry verticals), §1c (overseas, equal weight) and §1d (tertiary/research institutes) surfaced no item that could be reliably placed inside the last-24-hour window. The nearest sector signals — Townsville City Council's AI waste-truck pedestrian-safety system (8 Jun 2026), the ACT FOGO multi-unit pilot expansion, ASCA's reopened InsightWise AI trial for councils, the NSW AI-in-planning / DAISY program, the Bloomberg Mayors AI Forum launched at CityLab 2026, and the C40 mayors' sustainable-data-centre pledge (23 Jun 2026) — all predate the window and were excluded to respect the 24-hour rule and avoid stale duplication. No paywalled items are logged. Today is Monday, so the §1e 50-council Saturday watchlist was not scanned. Sections are published empty per §6.
